What is Program Testing?
opts: intended to show that a program does what it is intended to do and to discover program defects before it is put into use.
defect testing can be deliberately obscure and need not reflect how the system is normally used.
the system to perform correctly using a given set of test cases that reflect the system’s expected use.
a: intended to show that a program does what it is intended to do and to discover program defects before it is put into use.

q: It is where the system is tested during development to discover bugs and defects.
It is where individual program units or object classes are tested.
It is where several individual units are integrated to create composite components.
At this level, you directly reuse objects from a library rather than writing the code yourself.

At this level, you reuse entire application systems.

This technique, the tester or the QA analyst will only check the functionality of the particular module or particular method or sometimes the entire application by providing the different test cases manually.

It shows how the design is organized into logically related groups of objects.

The test environment copy the production setup where the software will run. This step involves
opts: Ensuring access to test data which is need for the project verification.
Configuring settings according to your project.
Installing the applications which is need for the testing purpose.
a: Ensuring access to test data which is need for the project verification.

q: Getting a product to market early may be more important than finding defects in the program.
It is where users or potential users of a system test the system in their own environment.
This means that there should be at least one test for every requirement in the requirements document.
